Lumen

AI-assisted biological image analysis, transforming raw microscopy TIFFs to
publication-ready quantification, on your local workstation.

Desktop Microscopy Platform
LUMEN01 / 16
Why Lumen
The Bottleneck in Modern
Biological Microscopy
Labs generate terabytes of fluorescence imaging data per week, yet every existing tool forces a painful tradeoff.
⚙️
ImageJ / Fiji

Manual & Fragile Pipelines

Requires expert-level macro scripting and manual threshold calibration per image modality. Watershed segmentation suffers from marker explosion in dense cell fields and marker starvation in sparse ones, offering no generalizable solution out of the box.

🧮
Classical CV

Heuristics That Don't Generalize

Otsu thresholding, Hough circle transforms, and morphological operations break down across imaging noise profiles, microscope brands, and staining protocols. Re-tuning parameters for every new dataset is not scalable science.

☁️
Enterprise SaaS

Cloud Lock-in & Cost Barriers

Cloud-based AI platforms push proprietary data formats, require internet connectivity for patient-sensitive microscopy data, and demand programming expertise just to invoke a model. Per-image pricing punishes high-throughput screening.

✦
Lumen solves this
Runs entirely local on your workstation. Auto-profiles every image on import, routes it to a validated deep learning model, and produces publication-ready metrics, with zero configuration and zero cloud dependency.

How It Works
Three Stages,
One Workstation
A seamless workflow from raw file import to publication-ready data export.
📤
Stage 1

Import & Normalization

Drag-and-drop ingestion. Heuristic classification determines modality while background thread worker auto-normalizes contrast.

📊
Stage 2

Interactive Analysis

Review segmentations on zoomable canvas. Click any cell to inspect, customize presets, or refine masks manually.

💾
Stage 3

Export & Reproducibility

Save color-coded masks, export CSV cell sheets, A4 PDF reports, or a complete JSON audit manifest for full reproducibility.

LUMEN04 / 18
Under The Hood · AI Engine
Cellpose: How the CNN Segments Cells
A generalizable model that utilizes horizontal and vertical spatial vector flows.

1. Vector Flow Prediction

Instead of simple thresholding, the model predicts a spatial gradient flow field, representing the direction each pixel should "flow" toward the center of its cell. This allows segmentation of highly irregular shapes.

2. Pixel Particle Dynamics

Pixels are treated as physical particles. An ordinary differential equation (ODE) solver tracks particle trajectories along the predicted flows, grouping them where they collide at cell centers.

3. Mask Reconstruction

Collided particle clusters define cell boundaries, reconstructed as unique integer masks. This approach guarantees zero overlaps and robustly separates touching cells.

Visual Representation
Raw Image Array[x, y, channel]
Horizontal Vector FlowdP/dx matrix
Vertical Vector FlowdP/dy matrix
Segmented Output Mask[x, y] = Cell ID
# Vector field mapping equation
v(x, y) = [ dP/dx, dP/dy ]
LUMEN05 / 18
Inside Lumen · Workspace
Import & Modality Detection
One drag-and-drop replaces the blank-canvas setup that most tools force on researchers.
Lumen · File Import
Instant

Pixel Array Profiling

Reads dimensions, channel count, and bit-depth the moment a file lands. No manual metadata entry is required.

Confidence-scored

Heuristic Classifier

A rule-based heuristic analyzes intensity distribution to score image modality, displayed with confidence before any model runs.

Ranked

Pipeline Recommendations

Suggests Cell Segmentation, Fluorescence Analysis, or Custom Workflows based on detected modality with relevance scores shown inline.

Design intent: A researcher who has never used Lumen should get a correctly-segmented output on their first image without reading any documentation.

LUMEN06 / 18
Inside Lumen · Analysis Canvas
Single Image Analysis Workspace
One canvas: raw grayscale TIFF in, labeled, queryable segmentation out. Single click.
QGraphicsView

Qt Graphics View Canvas

Hardware-accelerated zoomable and pannable canvas. Mask opacity slider adjusts overlay transparency in real time with no re-render required.

Interactive

Click-to-Inspect Cells

Click any segmented cell for an instant tooltip showing ID, area, diameter, and centroid coordinate. Per-cell data is queryable immediately.

Adaptive

Quality Presets

Choose between Balanced (standard) or Sensitive (sparse, dim populations). The Cellpose engine re-runs seamlessly in the background.

QThread

QThread Worker Pattern

Inference runs on a separate thread using explicit signals. The GUI remains responsive even during multi-minute CPU fallback processing.

Lumen · Image Analysis
LUMEN07 / 18
Inside Lumen · Batch Engine
Folder-Scale Batch Processing
Point Lumen at a folder and walk away; the pipeline scales from 1 to 200+ images with zero supervision.
Lumen · Batch Processing
Non-blocking

Non-Blocking Worker Thread

Cellpose inference runs in the background. The GUI progress view, live log, and cancel controls remain fully responsive during active runs.

Resume-safe

Intelligent Skip Detection

Pre-existing results trigger SKIPPED_ALREADY_EXISTS. An interrupted batch picks up exactly where it left off, avoiding redundant compute.

Memory-managed

Per-Iteration Garbage Collection

Image arrays are explicitly garbage collected from memory after export. Prevents memory leaks and VRAM spikes during massive runs.

State machine

Strict Lifecycle State Machine

Strict FSM transitions (IDLE -> RUNNING -> PAUSED -> IDLE). Every event call is atomic to ensure no state corruption occurs on cancel.

LUMEN08 / 18
Inside Lumen · Output
Results Explorer & Visualizer
Interactive dashboard to review, search, and visually inspect colored cell masks.
Dashboard

Interactive Batch Explorer

Search results by filename, sort alphabetically or by status. Quick list view ensures easy navigation through hundreds of processed images.

Visualizer

Color-Coded Overlay Visualizer

Live graphics view displaying color-coded cell masks with adjustable opacity. Inspect cell boundaries directly from the batch explorer.

Integration

Seamless Workspace Sync

Click "Open in Analysis Workspace" to load any batch result image back into the single-image explorer for manual corrections.

Reproducibility

Reproducibility Manifest

Every JSON manifest captures: model name, diameter setting, flow threshold, cellprob threshold, Cellpose version, and timestamp. Re-running any analysis 6 months later produces byte-identical output.

LUMEN10 / 18
Under The Hood · Architecture
Modular System Architecture
Flat, single-responsibility modules: GUI, processing, and persistence layers are cleanly separated.
app/ Bootstraps QApplication, env config, SQLite DB on launch
core/ Hardware backend detection, GPU/CPU fallback, theme singletons
core/services/ Navigation controller, Qt signal bus, session state
storage/ SQLite schema: window geometry, theme, last-opened folder
workflows/ Pipeline registry & task runner (Cellpose, normalization)
Lumen · Class Diagram
LUMEN11 / 18
Under The Hood · Architecture
System Design Principles
How Lumen enforces reliability, thread-safety, and extensibility.
🔬

Scientific Reliability First

Validated deep-learning frameworks over unproven classical heuristics. Cellpose version pinning prevents silent behavior shifts in segmentations across library updates.

# config.json (Strict Version Manifest)
{ "model": "cyto3", "version": "3.0.1", "deterministic_seed": 42 }
📐

Simple > Clever

Flat module hierarchy, explicit Qt signals, and structured JSON logs. Every control path is traceable in a standard debugger without navigating deep abstract wrappers.

GUI Event → Qt.QueuedConnection → MainController → QThread Worker
🔌

Extensible by Design

Unified interface wrappers decouple PySide6 GUI elements from processing models. Adding a new segmentation backend (e.g. StarDist) requires zero changes to the GUI layer.

class BaseModel(ABC):
  def segment(self, image: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
🧵

Explicit Thread-Safety

Enforced worker boundaries using explicit signals. This ensures worker threads never directly manipulate PySide6 widgets, preventing low-level C++ memory access violations and application crashes.

self.worker.finished.connect(self.on_success, Qt.QueuedConnection)

LUMEN13 / 18
Status
Production Stable v0.2.0
An honest snapshot of what's proven: tested coverage, known limitations, and what's next.
✓ Validated

What's Proven

✓End-to-end pipeline on single- and multi-channel 16-bit DAPI / GFP fluorescence TIFFs
✓60 unit tests covering normalization, heuristics, DB layer, manual segmentation, persistent batch inference
✓Sequential batch loop stress-tested across 200-image runs without memory leaks or instability
✓Thread-safety pattern validated: QueuedConnection on all worker signals eliminates cross-thread crashes
✓CUDA GPU path and CPU fallback both validated end-to-end
60
Unit tests passing
200+
Images batch-validated
⚠ Known

Limitations in Progress

△Pause/Resume throughput dip: first image after resuming shows brief latency spike; root cause under investigation.
△Manual mask editor overlap: toolbar can overlap at non-default window sizes; layout polish pending for extreme aspect ratios.
△StarDist not wired: routing hooks exist and the interface is defined, but the backend thread worker isn't connected yet.
△Single-channel only: multichannel visualization is the next planned phase; current pipeline processes one channel at a time.

🏗️ Current Development Focus

Completing the fluorescence quantification pipeline with per-channel mean intensity normalization, fixing the Pause/Resume latency issue, and wiring StarDist as the second segmentation backend.

What's Next · Roadmap
The Road to a Complete Analysis Platform
Five phases evolve Lumen from a segmentation tool into a full AI-powered biological image analysis platform.
PHASE 1 · In Progress
Fluorescence Pipeline
• Mean fluorescence per cell
• Integrated density calculation
• Rolling-ball background subtract
• Co-localization coefficients
• Multi-marker CSV schema
Completing now
PHASE 2 · Planned
Puncta Detection
• Sub-cellular vesicles & foci
• LoG blob detection algorithm
• Per-cell count & puncta area
• Nearest-neighbor to nucleus
• Puncta map overlays & CSV
Planned
PHASE 3 · Planned
3D Z-Stack Analysis
• Multi-plane TIFF loading
• 3D Cellpose across Z-planes
• Volume rendering (napari/VTK)
• Volumetric sphericity index
• Max/sum Z-projection modes
Planned
PHASE 4 · Planned
Time-Series Tracking
• Timelapse sequences loading
• Tracking via Hungarian solver
• Track divisions & velocity
• Lineage tree visualization
• Frame-by-frame CSV exports
Planned
PHASE 5 · Vision
AI-First Assistant
• Natural language queries
• Local LLM automated reports
• Flag anomalous distributions
• Fine-tuning UI for custom cells
• Local offline privacy inference
Vision
LUMEN15 / 18
The Vision
From Desktop Tool to
Global Research Infrastructure
The end state: a local AI platform that gives every researcher worldwide access to publication-grade image analysis: no cloud, no cost, no expertise barrier.
🔬

1. Any Modality (Input)

• Modality Detection: Auto-detects Fluorescence, Brightfield, Confocal, Phase contrast, and EM.
• Zero Config: Instantly profiles file pixel arrays and auto-routes to correct backend.
🤖

2. AI-First (Processing)

• Model Ensemble: Chains Cellpose, StarDist, and custom models for publication-grade segmentations.
• AI Assist: Semantic natural language dataset queries and auto-generated scientific reports.
🌍

3. Democratized (Impact)

• 100% Offline: Run entirely on local workstation GPU or CPU fallback. Zero cloud cost.
• Global Equity: Giving low-resource research labs access to world-class analysis.
“
The Core Thesis
Modern biological microscopy produces datasets that no human can analyze by hand. The bottleneck is not data collection, but quantification. Lumen's goal is to make that bottleneck disappear: drop in your TIFFs, get publication-ready numbers out, with a complete audit trail of exactly how the machine made every decision.
”
LUMEN16 / 18
Advanced Integrations · Planned
StarDist, OME-TIFF & Lab Infrastructure
Connecting Lumen to the wider scientific ecosystem. Phase 2-3 integrations that connect Lumen to standard lab pipelines.
🌌

StarDist Backend

StarDist models star-convex polygons, optimal for densely packed spherical nuclei where Cellpose's vector fields can overlap.

📄

OME-TIFF Metadata

OME-TIFF is the open standard for bioimaging metadata, embedding pixel calibration, channel names, and Z/T coordinates in a single file.

🧬

OMERO Integration

Push masks, CSV data, and run manifests directly to a lab's OMERO server, enabling multi-user, multi-experiment data management.

🚀
Unified Bioimaging Ecosystem

Every planned integration: StarDist, OME-TIFF, OMERO sync, and LLM reports, all running locally and reproducibly, is engineered to bridge raw microscopy TIFFs to publication-ready quantification.
